title: Beginner Athlete
principles: Keep weekly structure predictable and repeatable
Focus on successful weeks not perfect weeks
Recovery is productive
plan_rules: 3-5 exercises per session for strength
Default to full-body or upper/lower split
Conservative intensity RPE 6-7 (3-4 RIR)
80/20 rule for endurance (no VO2max first 4 weeks)
Leave room for missed sessions

When applying volume-to-time scaling, always use the lower end of the exercise range. Hard cap at 5 exercises per session regardless of duration or session composition. Default rest periods to 60-90 seconds (loads are lighter, recovery demands are lower).
hard_constraints: Max 4 training days/week
No VO2max work first 4 weeks endurance
RPE 6-7 for strength
nutrition_rules: Start with fundamentals: consistent protein, hydration, regular meals
Calorie target + protein target; macros optional
coaching_tone: Encouraging, clear, non-judgmental, educational without being overwhelming
evidence_sources: Helms et al. Muscle & Strength Pyramids
rationale: Beginners benefit most from sustainable structure. Slightly underdosed plans that get completed beat optimal plans that get abandoned.

title: Intermediate Athlete
principles: Progression with structure
Better fatigue management
Goal specificity
More intentional workload distribution
plan_rules: Double progression or undulating periodization
3-4 week mesocycles with deload every 4th week
10-18 sets/week for strength
Polarized 80/20 for endurance
RPE 7-8 (2-3 RIR)
RIR-based autoregulation
When applying volume-to-time scaling, use the full range from the corresponding session composition table. Standard rest periods as specified in the VOLUME_TIME_SCALING module.
hard_constraints: Deload every 4th week
Volume 10-18 sets/week per muscle
RPE 7-8 working sets
nutrition_rules: Goal-specific: Bulk +10-20%, Cut -15-25%, Maintain near TDEE
coaching_tone: Collaborative, specific, slightly more analytical, still practical
rationale: Intermediates benefit from structured periodization and more nuanced programming than beginners.

title: Advanced Athlete
principles: Specificity
Recovery management
Precision
Small changes matter more than dramatic changes
plan_rules: Highly goal/sport-specific
Respect accumulated fatigue
Block periodization may be appropriate
12-22+ sets/week per muscle
RPE 8-9 (1-2 RIR)
Reactive deloading

When applying volume-to-time scaling, can push toward the upper end of the exercise range. For primary compound lifts at near-maximal intensity, rest periods may extend to 180-300 seconds — this reduces total exercise count for a given duration but is appropriate because advanced athletes derive more benefit from intensity management than exercise variety.
hard_constraints: Volume 12-22+ sets/week (approaching MRV with caution)
RPE 8-9 key sets
No aggressive changes without user feedback evidence
coaching_tone: Respectful, precise, less explanatory fluff, more performance-oriented but still grounded
rationale: Advanced athletes need precision programming. Smaller improvements take more specificity and careful recovery management.

title: Age Group 18-29
training_adjustments: Build strong aerobic base and foundational strength
rationale: Young adults have peak recovery capacity and can tolerate higher training volumes while building foundational fitness.

title: Age Group 30-39
rationale: Adults in their 30s begin to see modest recovery changes and benefit from proactive warm-up and structured deloads.

title: Age Group 40-49
nutrition_rules: Recovery protein timing 20-40g within 1-2 hrs post-training
training_adjustments: Joint-friendly alternatives proactively offered (trap bar DL, incline press, goblet squat)
evidence_sources: Phillips et al. 2016 protein with aging
rationale: Athletes in their 40s benefit from joint-friendly alternatives, longer warm-ups, and higher protein intake to support recovery and connective tissue health.

title: Age Group 50-59
nutrition_rules: Protein 1.6-2.2 g/kg default (up to 2.4 g/kg in deficit/heavy training)
training_adjustments: 10-15 min warm-up with joint circles and progressive loading
rationale: Athletes 50-59 need longer warm-ups, bone density emphasis, balance work, and more recovery time between hard sessions.

title: Age Group 60+
nutrition_rules: Protein 1.6-2.2 g/kg default (up to 2.4 g/kg in deficit)
training_adjustments: Resistance training essential 2-3x/week for sarcopenia prevention
coaching_tone: Encouraging, respectful of experience, never patronizing. Focus on what they CAN do.
rationale: Older adults require sarcopenia prevention, fall prevention, functional movements, and careful recovery management with higher protein and micronutrient attention.

title: Male Gender Defaults
training_adjustments: Strength norms from male population data
Upper body emphasis natural but correct imbalances
rationale: Male-specific physiological norms for TDEE calculation, recovery windows, and strength benchmarks.

title: Female Gender Defaults
training_adjustments: Higher fatigue resistance at moderate intensities (more reps at given %1RM)
Menstrual cycle-aware programming opt-in (follicular=higher intensity, luteal=moderate + higher volume -- symptom-driven)
ACL prevention: knee stability, hamstring strengthening
Higher relative recovery for repeated moderate sets
rationale: Female-specific physiological considerations including menstrual cycle awareness, ACL injury prevention, bone density, and iron intake.

title: Gender-Neutral Defaults
nutrition_rules: Activity-adjusted TDEE without sex-specific multipliers (or average)
coaching_tone: Inclusive, non-gendered language. Focus on individual data over population assumptions.
rationale: Gender-neutral approach using bodyweight-relative norms and individual recovery signals rather than sex-specific population data.

title: Strength Training Core
principles: Volume landmarks (Israetel/RP): Large muscles MEV 6-8, MAV 12-18, MRV 20-25+
Small muscles MEV 4-6, MAV 8-12, MRV 15-20
2x/week frequency per muscle optimal
8-12 reps most efficient (stimulus-to-fatigue)
Rest: 3-5 min heavy compounds, 90-120s accessories
plan_rules: Double progression (add reps then weight)
Linear for beginners
RPE/RIR-based for intermediate+
Split design: 2-3 days=full body, 3-4=UL or FB, 4-5=UL or PPL, 5-6=PPL
Proactive deload every 4th week (reduce volume 40-50%, intensity 10-15%)
Exercise rotation: Tier 1 stable 4-6 weeks, Tier 2 rotate 2-4 weeks, Tier 3 rotate 1-3 weeks

Before assembling exercises for any session, classify the session as compound-heavy, isolation-heavy, or mixed based on the planned movement pattern distribution. Use the VOLUME_TIME_SCALING module's corresponding table to determine exercise count and rest period targets for the requested session duration.
hard_constraints: Proximity to failure: beginners 3-4 RIR, intermediate 2-3 RIR, advanced 1-2 RIR
rationale: Core strength training module defining volume landmarks, progression models, split design, and deload protocols based on current evidence.
change_log: 2026-04-08 v1.1: Added dependency on VOLUME_TIME_SCALING module for session assembly

title: Time-aware volume scaling for session duration
principles: Session duration is a hard constraint, not a suggestion — workouts must fill the requested time window
Under-filling a session erodes trust and wastes adaptation potential
Over-filling creates time pressure that degrades form and RPE accuracy
Compound-heavy sessions require more rest and fewer exercises per unit time than isolation sessions
Advanced athletes may need fewer exercises with longer rest — intensity trumps variety at high levels
plan_rules: Classify each session as compound-heavy (≥60% compounds), isolation-heavy (≥60% isolation), or mixed
Use the corresponding volume-to-time table to determine exercise count range for the requested duration
Apply level modifier: beginners use lower end of range (cap 5), intermediates use full range, advanced can push upper end
Calculate estimated session time using: warm_up + Σ(sets × time_per_set) + (exercises × 1.5min transition) + 5min buffer
Reject plans where estimated time is <70% or >110% of requested duration
hard_constraints: Minimum 2 exercises per session regardless of duration
Maximum 12 exercises per session regardless of duration
Beginners capped at 5 exercises per session
Compound exercises: minimum 120s rest between sets
Session time coverage must be 70-110% of requested duration
coaching_tone: Confident, evidence-based. When explaining why a session has fewer or more exercises than expected, reference the session composition and rest requirements.
evidence_sources: Schoenfeld et al. (2017) — Dose-response relationship between weekly resistance training volume and muscle mass
Grgic et al. (2017) — Effects of short versus long inter-set rest intervals on muscle hypertrophy
Schoenfeld et al. (2021) — Loading recommendations re-examination of the repetition continuum
ACSM Guidelines for Exercise Testing and Prescription, 11th ed.
NSCA Essentials of Strength Training and Conditioning, 4th ed.
rationale: Without time-aware volume scaling, the engine produces workouts that drastically under-fill or over-fill the requested duration. This module ensures every workout plausibly fills the user's available time by mapping duration × session composition × level to concrete volume parameters.

title: Endurance Running Core
principles: Polarized 80/20 (80% Z1-2, 20% Z4-5, minimal Z3)
plan_rules: Volume progression context-based (easy weeks +10-15%, hard weeks +5-8%)
Cutback week every 4-6 weeks (drop 20-30%)
Never add volume AND intensity same week
For runners with a specific time goal: include one weekly interval session (800m or 1K repeats at I-pace). This is non-negotiable for time-goal training.
Total interval volume should be 5-8% of weekly mileage for intermediates, up to 10% for advanced
Place interval sessions on a day following an easy day, never after a long run or tempo
Progress repeats from shorter (400-800m) to longer (1K-1600m) as the race approaches
Reference RUNNING_ZONE_CALCULATIONS module for pacing interval work by HR zones
Insert a threshold week into every training plan at the cadence defined by TRAINING_THRESHOLD_WEEK (4/6/8 weeks based on training phase)
During a threshold week, the week's only hard effort is the 5K test (or 30-min fallback) on Day 4. All other days are Z1 with optional strides.
After a threshold week test, regenerate all subsequent workout zones from the new VDOT via RUNNING_ZONE_CALCULATIONS.
When the user adds a custom session via the Add Running Session flow, generate the workout via RUNNING_WORKOUT_GENERATOR using SESSION_TYPE_LIBRARY templates
Respect SESSION_TYPE_LIBRARY hard_constraints (max 3 hard sessions/week, no back-to-back hard, no hard after long run)
Trigger weekly_stress_check on every save (replace or add path)
If a custom session replaces a planned workout, call planner.rebalanceWeek to redistribute volume across remaining days
evidence_sources: Billat et al. — vVO2max interval research
Midgley et al. (2006) — systematic review on VO2max training
rationale: Polarized training distribution and context-dependent volume progression form the foundation of evidence-based running programming. Interval repeats for time-goal runners develop VO2max and race-specific fitness.
change_log: 2026-04-08: Added weekly interval repeat requirement for time-goal runners; linked to zone calculations module. 2026-04-09: Added threshold week scheduling and post-test zone regeneration for run-only athletes. 2026-04-09: Added Add Running Session generator integration with SESSION_TYPE_LIBRARY and weekly stress check.

title: 5K Race Plan
plan_rules: Duration: beginner 8-10 weeks, intermediate 6-8, advanced 4-6
Key sessions: 1x tempo 20-40min Z3-Z4, 1x VO2max intervals (5x1000m at 5K pace), 1x progression long run 6-9mi, 1-2x easy runs
Mileage: beginner 15-25mpw, intermediate 25-35, advanced 35-50
Taper 5-7 days, reduce 40-60% maintain intensity
rationale: 5K-specific training plan with distance-appropriate key sessions, mileage ranges by level, and short taper protocol.

title: 10K Race Plan
plan_rules: Duration: beginner 10-12 weeks, intermediate 8-10, advanced 6-8
Key sessions: 1x 10K intervals (5x1mi at 10K pace), 1x threshold (2-3x8-10min at LT), 1x progression long 8-12mi, easy runs
Mileage: beginner 20-30, intermediate 30-45, advanced 45-70
Taper 5-7 days, reduce 40-50%
rationale: 10K-specific training plan with threshold and interval sessions, progressive long runs, and appropriate mileage ranges.

title: Half Marathon Race Plan
plan_rules: 12-14 weeks
Periodization: Base (wk1-4) aerobic 5-8mi long. Build (wk5-9) tempo+intervals long to 12mi. Peak (wk10-11) race-pace 12-13mi. Taper (wk12+) 2-3 weeks 40-60% reduction
Key sessions: 1x HM pace 10-13mi, 1x tempo 25-35min Z3, 1x 5K/10K-pace intervals, 1x long with HM segments
Mileage: beginner 25-40, intermediate 40-60, advanced 65-95
Race nutrition: gel every 45-60min >75min
rationale: Half marathon plan with phased periodization, race-specific long runs, and introduction of race-day nutrition strategy.

title: Marathon Race Plan
plan_rules: 16-20 weeks

Three approaches: Pfitzinger (5-phase, peak 55-85mpw, MP long runs, 3-week taper 70/55/30%), Hansons (cumulative fatigue, 6 days/week, max long 16mi, 3 quality sessions, peak 50-55mpw), Traditional (base/build/peak/taper, long to 18-20mi, 3 quality/week, 3-week taper)
Taper 21 days optimal, volume -41-60%, maintain intensity
Pacing: percentage-based, first 5K 10-15sec/mile slower
Race nutrition: carb load 24-48hr 8-12g/kg, during 60-90g carbs/hr from first 15min
Scale the number of 20+ mile runs by runner experience and weekly mileage: first-timers 0-1, beginners 1-2, intermediates 2-4, advanced 4-5 with 6-12 miles at marathon pace per long run
Never program more than one 20+ mile run per 2-week period
Minimum 10-14 days between 20+ milers for all levels
rationale: Marathon plan offering three proven methodologies with evidence-based taper protocols, experience-scaled long run programming, and critical race-day nutrition strategy.
change_log: 2026-04-08: Added experience-scaled marathon long run programming with safety guardrails

title: Ultramarathon Race Plan
plan_rules: 16-24 weeks
Emphasis time-on-feet over speed
Long runs 3-5+ hours
Mandatory cutback every 3-4 weeks
Phases: Base (wk1-8) build to 20mi. Specific (9-16) 20-28+mi with walking. Intensity (17-20) peak 25-32mi. Taper (21-24) progressive reduction
Nutrition: 150-400 kcal/hr, real food OK, practice extensively
rationale: Ultra training prioritizes time-on-feet and nutrition practice over speed, with extended build phases and mandatory cutbacks.

title: Cycling Training
principles: Coggan power zones: Z1 <55% FTP, Z2 56-75%, Z3 76-90% (sweet spot 89-93%), Z4 91-105%, Z5 106-120%
plan_rules: Key workouts: sweet spot 2x20min 90% FTP, VO2max 5x5min 108% FTP, over-unders 90%/105% alternating, endurance 1-4hr Z2
Periodization: Base 8-12wk Z2 to Build 6-8wk Z3-5 to Peak 3-4wk event-specific
3 weeks hard 1 week recovery
Volume: beginner 5-6hr/wk, intermediate 8-10, advanced 12-16+
rationale: Power-zone-based cycling training with structured periodization from base through peak phases.

title: Swimming Training
plan_rules: CSS-based zones (200m+400m test): Recovery 65-75% CSS, Endurance 75-90%, Threshold 90-100%, VO2max 100-110%
Session structure: warm-up 10-15%, drills 10-15%, main 50-60%, kick/pull 10-20%, cool-down
Volume: beginner 3000-3500m/wk, intermediate 4500-6000m, competitive 6000-8000+
Max 10%/week increase
rationale: CSS-based swimming training with structured session composition and volume progression limits.

title: Triathlon Training
plan_rules: Volume distribution: swim 10-12%, bike 50-55%, run 30-35%
Brick workouts 1-2x/week (sprint 30min+10min, olympic 60+20, 70.3 90-120+30-45)
Periodization: traditional or reverse (reverse better for swim)
3wk build + 1wk recovery
Taper: sprint 7-10d, olympic 10-14d, 70.3 14-21d, ironman 21-28d
All taper reduce 40-60% maintain intensity
Concurrent training: min 3hr gap strength/endurance same day, strength first when fresh, max 2 HIIT/week, bike/row for HIIT
Insert a threshold week into every triathlon training plan at the cadence defined by TRAINING_THRESHOLD_WEEK (4/6/8 weeks based on training phase)
During a triathlon threshold week, schedule all three tests in the order swim -> bike -> run, one test per day, with at least one easy day separation between tests
Default test placement: swim CSS on Thursday, bike FTP on Friday or Saturday, run 5K on Saturday or Sunday. Never stack two tests on the same day.
After each test in the threshold week, immediately regenerate that sport's zones so the next test is paced against the most current fitness data

If the user is a triathlete with one weak discipline, allow that discipline's zones to be recalibrated independently more often (e.g., a strong cyclist who wants to retest their run every 4 weeks while keeping bike at every 6)
rationale: Multi-sport triathlon training with sport-specific volume distribution, brick workouts, and distance-dependent taper protocols.
change_log: 2026-04-09: Added triathlon-specific threshold week structure with all three tests spread across the week. Per-discipline cadence override allowed.

title: HIIT Training
plan_rules: Formats: circuit (30-45s work, multi-exercise), Tabata (20s/10s x8, advanced only max 1-2x/wk), EMOM, AMRAP
Max 2-3 sessions/week (never exceed 3 with strength)
Work:rest ratios: fat loss 1:3 to 1:1, performance 2:1 to 3:1, VO2max 1:1 to 1:2
4-week blocks: wk1-2 build volume, wk3 peak intensity, wk4 deload
nutrition_rules: Pre-HIIT fast carbs 60-90min before (fasted HIIT no fat loss advantage per 2024-2025 reviews)
rationale: HIIT programming with format variety, frequency limits, goal-specific work:rest ratios, and 4-week block structure.

title: Bodyweight Training
plan_rules: Progressive overload without weight: easier-to-harder variations, increased reps/sets (progress 1-2 reps/wk, harder variation at 12-15 reps), decreased rest, increased ROM, eccentric loading (3-5s negatives), tempo manipulation
Cover all patterns 2-3x/week: horizontal push/pull, vertical push/pull, squat, hinge, core, carry
Volume: strength 10-15 sets/wk per pattern, hypertrophy 15-25
evidence_sources: Progressive push-up training comparable to bench press when volume-matched
rationale: Bodyweight training using progressive variation, movement pattern coverage, and evidence showing comparable results to weighted training when volume-matched.

title: Yoga and Mobility
plan_rules: Session: dynamic warm-up 2-3min, main mobility 10-15min (3-4 exercises per region), cool-down 2-3min

Stretching: dynamic pre-workout only (10-12 reps), static post-workout or dedicated days (30-60s hold, 2-4 reps, NEVER before strength), PNF most effective (3-6s contraction then 10-30s stretch 1-2x/wk)
Frequency: 2-3x/week minimum
Dynamic pre (5-10min) + static post (10-15min) + 1-2 dedicated days (20-45min)
Daily 10-15min mobility recommended
rationale: Mobility and flexibility programming with evidence-based stretching protocols and timing considerations relative to strength training.

title: Rowing Training
plan_rules: HR-based zones
Key workouts: steady state 30-80min Z2, intervals 4x500m threshold, rate work
Volume: beginner 3-4 sessions/week 20-30min, intermediate 4 sessions 30-45min
rationale: HR-based rowing training with steady state and interval key workouts scaled by experience level.

title: Pilates Training
plan_rules: Treat as mobility + core strength
2-3x/week
Complement strength training
Focus on movement quality not intensity
rationale: Pilates as a complementary modality focusing on core strength and movement quality.

title: Walking
plan_rules: Low-intensity steady state
Counts toward daily movement not training load
Encourage as active recovery
No periodization needed
rationale: Walking as a low-intensity daily movement and active recovery modality, not counted as structured training load.

title: Sport-Specific Performance
plan_rules: Build around competition calendar
Prioritize sport movement patterns
Strength supports sport
Recovery becomes more dynamic
rationale: Sport-specific training organized around competition calendars with strength work supporting primary sport demands.

title: Muscle Gain / Bulk
plan_rules: Hypertrophy rep ranges 6-15
Progressive overload
Volume progression
nutrition_rules: Carbs 4-7 g/kg higher on training days
Body comp check every 4-6 weeks (if >60% fat gain, reduce surplus)
rationale: Muscle gain phase with controlled surplus, high protein distribution, and body composition monitoring to minimize fat gain.

title: Fat Loss with Muscle Preservation
nutrition_rules: Carbs around training, reduce rest days
Duration 8-16 weeks max then 4+ weeks maintenance
rationale: Evidence-based cutting protocol prioritizing muscle preservation through elevated protein, maintained intensity, and strategic refeeds.

title: General Weight Loss
nutrition_rules: Portion awareness, protein each meal, hydration
NO 'burning off meals' or 'earning food' language
rationale: Sustainable weight loss approach emphasizing consistency, resistance training, adequate protein, and healthy relationship with food.

title: Maintenance / General Health
nutrition_rules: Monitor body composition not just scale
Higher-load resistance training superior for recomp
rationale: Maintenance and recomposition phase focusing on body composition improvement at maintenance calories with progressive overload.

title: Performance Goal
plan_rules: Race/event-specific periodization
Taper logic
Peak training blocks
Sport-specific volume distribution
nutrition_rules: No deficit during peak training blocks
rationale: Performance-focused goal prioritizing event-specific periodization and adequate fueling over body composition goals.

title: Strength Nutrition by Goal
nutrition_rules: Bulk: +10-20% TDEE, protein 1.6-2.2 g/kg, carbs 4-7 g/kg, fat 0.5-1.5 g/kg
Cut: -15-25% TDEE, protein 1.6-2.4 g/kg, carbs 2-4 g/kg, fat 0.5-1.0 g/kg, refeed 1-2x/week
Weight loss: -20-30% TDEE, protein 1.4-2.0, carbs 2-4, fat 0.5-1.0
rationale: Goal-specific macro targets for strength athletes across bulk, cut, maintenance, and weight loss phases.

title: Endurance Nutrition by Distance
nutrition_rules: 5K/10K: carbs 3-5 g/kg, protein 1.4-1.8, no carb loading, no during-race fuel needed
Half marathon: carbs 5-7, protein 1.4-1.8, 24hr carb load 7-8 g/kg, gel every 45-60min >75min, 150-300ml/20min
Marathon: carbs 7-10 peak, protein 1.4-2.0, carb load 24-48hr 8-12 g/kg, 60-90g carbs/hr from first 15min, sodium 500-700mg/hr
Ultra: carbs 8-12 peak, protein 1.4-2.0, 48hr carb load, 150-400 kcal/hr real food OK, sodium+electrolytes
rationale: Distance-specific nutrition protocols scaling carbohydrate needs, race fueling, and hydration strategies from 5K through ultra distances.

title: Cycling and Triathlon Nutrition
nutrition_rules: Cycling: carbs 3-5 easy days, 8-12 hard days
Triathlon: 73% Ironman DNFs trace to nutrition
rationale: Cycling and triathlon-specific nutrition emphasizing carb periodization by training day intensity and critical race-day fueling protocols.

title: HIIT and Bodyweight Nutrition
nutrition_rules: Pre-HIIT: fast carbs 60-90min before (fasted HIIT no advantage)
rationale: HIIT and bodyweight training nutrition with pre/post timing protocols and daily macro targets.

title: Yoga, Mobility, and Walking Nutrition
nutrition_rules: Lower intensity does NOT mean low protein
rationale: Nutrition for lower-intensity modalities emphasizing that protein needs remain substantial regardless of training intensity.

title: Universal Hydration Guidelines
nutrition_rules: Endurance events: sport-specific protocols by duration/environment/sweat rate
rationale: Evidence-based hydration approach favoring thirst-driven intake over rigid formulas, with sport-specific adjustments for endurance events.

title: Exercise Rotation Protocol
plan_rules: Tier 1 primary compounds: stable 4-6 weeks per mesocycle (squat, bench, deadlift, OHP, row)
Tier 2 secondary: rotate every 2-4 weeks (incline bench, DB press, front squat, RDL, cable row)
Tier 3 accessories: rotate every 1-3 weeks (lateral raises, curls, tricep work)
Between mesocycles: swap Tier 2 and all Tier 3
Keep Tier 1 unless plateau
rationale: Tiered exercise rotation balancing movement stability for progression tracking with sufficient variation to prevent accommodation.

title: Rep Range Undulation
plan_rules: 4-week mesocycle: Week 1: 4x8-10 RPE 7 (hypertrophy accumulation)
Week 2: 5x5-6 RPE 8 (strength emphasis)
Week 3: 3x10-12 RPE 8-9 (metabolic stress)
Week 4: 3x8 RPE 5-6 (deload, technique focus)
rationale: Weekly rep range undulation within a mesocycle targeting different training stimuli while managing fatigue through a built-in deload.

title: Running Workout Variation
plan_rules: Each week different workout types, not same run repeated
Easy runs (Z2) 2-3 days (similar week to week)
Quality 1: varies by phase (base=steady-state, build=tempo/threshold, peak=race-pace)
Quality 2: intervals (800m, 1200m, fartlek, hills rotate)
Long run: Week 1 all easy, Week 2 last 3mi tempo, Week 3 progression, Week 4 cutback easy
rationale: Running variation protocol ensuring weekly workout diversity while maintaining consistent easy run volume and phase-appropriate quality sessions.

title: HIIT Workout Variation
plan_rules: Rotate formats: circuit, Tabata, EMOM, AMRAP
Rotate focus: full body, upper, lower, core, cardio-heavy
Rotate equipment: bodyweight, dumbbells, kettlebell, full gym
Vary work:rest as periodization progresses
rationale: HIIT variation across format, focus area, equipment, and work:rest ratios to prevent staleness and drive adaptation.

title: Mesocycle and Macrocycle Structure
plan_rules: Mesocycle (4 weeks): Wk1 intro/moderate below target, Wk2 build at target progressing, Wk3 peak highest volume/intensity, Wk4 deload -40-50% volume
Macrocycle (12-16 weeks): Block 1 (1-4) hypertrophy, Block 2 (5-8) strength, Block 3 (9-12) peak/power, Block 4 (13-16) active recovery/transition
Between blocks: swap Tier 2+3 exercises, adjust rep schemes, potentially swap split
rationale: Structured mesocycle and macrocycle periodization providing systematic volume/intensity progression with built-in recovery and phase transitions.

title: Safety Boundaries and Constraints
hard_constraints: No diagnosis/treatment/medical nutrition therapy
Calorie floors 1200F/1500M
Protein floor 0.6 g/lb
RED-S detection <800cal 3+ days
No prohibited phrases (guaranteed results, lose X in Y days, burn off meal, cure, treat, diagnose)
Injury escalation: persistent pain -> professional referral
Pregnancy/chronic conditions: conservative + referral
Max progression 10-15%/wk endurance, 2-4 sets/wk strength
rationale: Safety and sustainability outrank optimization. These are non-negotiable.

title: Good Recovery State
principles: Athlete is recovering well between sessions
plan_rules: Proceed with planned progression
Normal volume and intensity targets
training_adjustments: Can push intensity on quality sessions
rationale: Placeholder module for good recovery state. To be expanded with recovery monitoring integration.

title: Moderate Recovery State
principles: Athlete showing some recovery deficit signs
plan_rules: Reduce volume 10-20% this week
Maintain intensity on key sessions
rationale: Placeholder module for moderate recovery state. To be expanded with recovery monitoring integration.

title: Low Recovery State
principles: Athlete showing significant recovery deficit
plan_rules: Trigger unscheduled deload
Reduce volume 40-50%
Reduce intensity to RPE 5-6
hard_constraints: No high-intensity sessions until recovery improves
rationale: Placeholder module for low recovery state. To be expanded with recovery monitoring integration.

title: General Injury Caution
principles: When in doubt, refer out
Pain is a signal, not a challenge to overcome
plan_rules: Avoid aggravating movements
Substitute with pain-free alternatives
Reduce load on affected area
hard_constraints: Persistent pain (>7 days) -> professional referral mandatory
No training through acute pain
training_adjustments: Work around injury, not through it
coaching_tone: Cautious, supportive, clear about limitations of non-medical advice
rationale: Placeholder module for general injury caution. To be expanded with injury-specific protocols and professional referral pathways.

title: Shoulder Injury Caution
principles: Shoulder complex requires careful loading progression
plan_rules: Avoid overhead pressing if painful
Substitute with landmine press, floor press, or neutral grip variations
Prioritize rotator cuff prehab/rehab work
hard_constraints: No behind-the-neck movements
No heavy overhead work without clearance
training_adjustments: Band pull-aparts and external rotation work daily
Reduce pulling volume if impingement symptoms
coaching_tone: Cautious, supportive, clear about limitations of non-medical advice
rationale: Placeholder module for shoulder injury caution. To be expanded with specific rehabilitation protocols and professional referral criteria.

title: Knee Injury Caution
principles: Knee health requires balanced quad/hamstring/glute strength
plan_rules: Reduce deep squat ROM if painful
Box squats, leg press with limited ROM as alternatives
Prioritize VMO strengthening and hamstring work
hard_constraints: No plyometrics or high-impact jumping
No deep single-leg loading without clearance
nutrition_rules: Collagen supplementation may support connective tissue (emerging evidence)
training_adjustments: Hip and glute strengthening to offload knee
Cycling or swimming for cardio instead of running
coaching_tone: Cautious, supportive, clear about limitations of non-medical advice
rationale: Placeholder module for knee injury caution. To be expanded with specific rehabilitation protocols and professional referral criteria.

title: Offseason / transition phase for runners
principles: Offseason volume: 50-60% of peak training volume
Sessions should be shorter (30-45 min most runs)
Focus shifts to speed development, hill work, and strength training
2-3 strength sessions/week — best time in the annual cycle to prioritize gym work
Encourage enjoyment and exploration — reduce watch dependency
plan_rules: Reduce weekly mileage to 50-60% of the user's peak training volume
No long runs exceeding 60 minutes
Include 2-3 stride sessions per week (6-10 x 80-100m near-sprint with full recovery)
Include 1 hill session per week (6-10 x 60-90s hard effort hill repeats)
Include 2-3 strength training sessions targeting compound lower body + single-leg work
No structured interval or tempo work — keep running aerobic and fun
Duration: 2-6 weeks depending on preceding training block intensity and race
hard_constraints: Never reduce volume below 40% of peak (risk of excessive detraining)
Never eliminate running entirely unless medically indicated
Strength training intensity can increase but running intensity should decrease
coaching_tone: Relaxed, encouraging exploration. Less pressure, more autonomy. 'This is your time to run without a watch.'
evidence_sources: Daniels — Daniels' Running Formula (transition phase guidance)
General coaching consensus on offseason volume reduction
rationale: The offseason allows physical and mental recovery while maintaining a fitness base. Speed and strength work during reduced volume builds qualities that are hard to develop during high-volume training blocks.

title: Tiered heart rate zone calculation system

recalculation_hooks.behavior: Recalculate all 5 pace zones and all 5 HR zones using the new input. Archive the prior zone snapshot to user_zone_history. Push a notification to the user comparing old vs new Z3 (threshold) boundary as a quick visual.

returns.hr_zones: array of 5 zones with bpm ranges

principles: Progressive accuracy: zones improve as user provides more data
Tier 1 (age only): Max HR = 208 - 0.7*age. Standard error +/-10 bpm.
Tier 2 (age + resting HR): Karvonen formula. Accounts for individual fitness level.
Tier 3 (LTHR): Zones as % of lactate threshold HR. Most accurate for trained runners.
Always use the highest tier available. If user provides known max HR, use it instead of age-predicted.
plan_rules: Use highest available zone tier when prescribing intensity
When resting HR is added, recalculate all zones and notify user of upgrade
Complement HR zones with pace zones from VDOT for cross-reference
hard_constraints: Never prescribe zones without at least age data
Display accuracy note for Tier 1 zones (age-estimated)
evidence_sources: Tanaka et al. (2001) — Age-predicted maximal heart rate revisited, JACC
Karvonen et al. (1957) — The effects of training on heart rate
ACSM Guidelines for Exercise Testing and Prescription, 11th ed.
rationale: Progressive accuracy: zones improve as user provides more data. No user is blocked by lack of lab testing, but serious runners get precise zones when they have the data.
change_log: 2026-04-09: Added recalculation_hooks field so that threshold week tests automatically refresh cached pace and HR zones. No change to existing zone math. 2026-04-09: Exposed getZonesForUser(userId) public API so the workout generator can pull zones in a single call. No change to zone math.

title: 1/3 feedback calibration rule for plan adjustment
principles: Post-hoc review tool, not a training prescription
~1/3 workouts should feel easier, ~1/3 about right, ~1/3 harder than intended
Assesses perceived effort relative to intent, NOT intensity distribution
Compatible with 80/20 polarized model — both frameworks coexist
plan_rules: After each completed workout, prompt: How did this feel compared to what you expected? (Easier / About right / Harder)
Aggregate over the week and surface calibration signals in weekly check-in
If >50% of workouts feel harder than intended for 2+ consecutive weeks: reduce volume 10% or intensity one notch
If >50% of workouts feel easier than intended for 2+ consecutive weeks: consider progression step
Single-week deviations are normal and should not trigger automatic adjustments
hard_constraints: This rule must never override Tier 1 evidence-based training prescriptions
Framed as feedback tool only — never as a training distribution target
evidence_sources: Compatible with Seiler (2010) polarized training model (Tier 1)
rationale: Provides a simple, user-friendly feedback mechanism that helps the app (and user) detect when training load is miscalibrated. Framed as a review tool, not a prescription, so it doesn't conflict with evidence-based programming rules.

title: Endurance Cycling Core
principles: Coggan power zones drive prescription: Z1 <55% FTP, Z2 56-75%, Z3 76-90%, Z4 91-105%, Z5 106-120%
Polarized 80/20 distribution applies to cycling as well — most riding is Z1-Z2
plan_rules: Insert a threshold week into every training plan at the cadence defined by TRAINING_THRESHOLD_WEEK (4/6/8 weeks based on training phase)
During a threshold week, the week's only hard effort is the 20-min FTP test on Day 4. All other days are Z1.
After a successful FTP test, regenerate all subsequent workout zones using Coggan's % FTP table.
hard_constraints: Never schedule a 20-min FTP test the day after a long ride or hard interval session
evidence_sources: Coggan, A. & Allen, H. — Training and Racing with a Power Meter
rationale: FTP-anchored training is the standard for cyclists. Bundling FTP retesting into a scheduled deload (the threshold week) keeps zones current without relying on the user to remember.
change_log: 2026-04-09: Module created with threshold week scheduling and post-test FTP zone regeneration for cyclists.

title: Endurance Swimming Core
principles: CSS (Critical Swim Speed) anchors the prescription. Easy = CSS + 12s/100m, threshold = CSS, race pace = CSS - 3 to 5s/100m.
Technique work belongs in every session — swimming is more technique-limited than fitness-limited for most triathletes
plan_rules: Insert a threshold week into every training plan at the cadence defined by TRAINING_THRESHOLD_WEEK (4/6/8 weeks based on training phase)
During a threshold week, the week's only hard effort is the CSS test (400m + 200m TT) on Day 4. All other days are Z1 technique-focused swimming.
After a successful CSS test, regenerate swim zones: easy = CSS + 12s/100m, threshold = CSS, race pace = CSS - 3 to 5s/100m.
hard_constraints: Never schedule a CSS test the day after a long swim or hard threshold session
evidence_sources: Olbrecht, J. — The Science of Winning
TrainingPeaks — Threshold Tests for Swim, Bike and Run (Hayden Scott)
rationale: CSS-anchored training is the most accessible field-test-driven swim system. Bundling the CSS test into a scheduled threshold week ensures zones stay current.
change_log: 2026-04-09: Module created with threshold week scheduling and post-test CSS zone regeneration for swimmers.

title: Threshold week protocol — periodic deload + fitness retesting
principles: A threshold week is a deload week that doubles as a fitness assessment.
Volume drops to 60-70% of the prior peak week and all hard work is removed except the test itself.
Test results auto-update VDOT (run), FTP (bike), and CSS (swim); zones recalculate immediately.
Skipping a threshold week slides the cadence forward — never compress.
plan_rules: Single-sport athletes get one test per threshold week placed on Day 4 (Thursday by default).
Triathletes get all three tests in the same threshold week, in order swim -> bike -> run, one per day, with at least one easy day between tests.
Total volume target for the week is 60-70% of the prior week's volume.
Zero workouts above Z2 during a threshold week except the test efforts themselves.
hard_constraints: Never schedule two threshold tests on the same day
Never schedule a test the day after a long run/ride/swim
Never insert a threshold week within 14 days of a goal A race
Never schedule two threshold weeks back-to-back (minimum 3 weeks build between)
Never allow more than 8 weeks to pass without a threshold week unless explicitly disabled by user
Abort the test and reschedule if user reports RPE > 8 on three consecutive days during the threshold week or reports illness/injury
Single-sport athletes get one test per threshold week; triathletes get all three spread across the week

race_prep.definition: Within 8 weeks of an A race
race_prep.rationale: Higher load + faster fitness gains require more frequent deloads and recalibration

base.definition: 8+ weeks from any A race, general training

maintenance.rationale: Lower load means less fatigue accumulation and slower zone drift

user_override.default_source: auto-detected from training_phase, can be manually set in settings

week_structure.intensity_rule: Zero work above Z2 except the test efforts themselves

run_5k.name: 5K Time Trial
run_5k.calculation: Lookup VDOT from 5K finish time using Daniels VDOT tables
run_5k.fallback: 30-minute run test (Friel): avg HR of last 20 min = LTHR, avg pace of last 20 min = T-pace. Use when 5K race effort is unsafe (injury return, masters, beginners).

bike_ftp_20min.name: 20-Minute FTP Test
bike_ftp_20min.warmup: 15 min easy + 3x1 min progressive (Z2->Z3->Z4) + 5 min easy + 5 min hard primer (~108% expected FTP) + 10 min easy
bike_ftp_20min.test: 20 min all-out at highest sustainable power
bike_ftp_20min.source: Coggan & Allen, Training and Racing with a Power Meter

swim_css.name: Critical Swim Speed Test
swim_css.test: 400m all-out TT, 5-10 min recovery, 200m all-out TT
swim_css.calculation: CSS = (T_400_seconds - T_200_seconds) / 2, expressed as sec per 100m
swim_css.source: Olbrecht; popularized in triathlon by TrainingPeaks

skip_behavior.description: If a threshold week is skipped, reschedule the NEXT threshold week to N weeks after the skip date, not N weeks after the originally planned date.
skip_behavior.rationale: Compressing two threshold weeks closer together creates an unintended high-recovery cluster and disrupts the build:recover ratio.

post_test_workflow: Prompt user to log test result immediately when test workout is marked complete
Sanity-check: flag if new value differs from prior by more than 15% (likely logging error or anomalous test day)
If valid, archive prior value to fitness_history table and write new value to user profile
Recalculate all cached training zones for that sport using the appropriate calculation module
Push notification to user with before/after Z3 boundary as a quick visual
Update all future workouts in the active plan to use the new zones

validation_rules.out_of_range_action: Show confirmation modal: 'This is a [X]% change from your last test. Confirm the result is correct, or retake the test.'

coaching_tone: Lower intensity, intentional. The week is rest plus an honest snapshot, not a peak week. Frame the test as a B race, not an A race.
evidence_sources: Friel, J. — A Quick Guide to Setting Zones (joefrieltraining.com)
Friel, J. — The Triathlete's Training Bible
Coggan, A. & Allen, H. — Training and Racing with a Power Meter (FTP test protocol)
Daniels, J. — Daniels' Running Formula (VDOT system + 5K-based calibration)
Olbrecht, J. — The Science of Winning (CSS protocol origins)
TrainingPeaks — Threshold Tests for Swim, Bike and Run (Hayden Scott)
Seiler, S. (2010) — Polarized training intensity distribution
Laursen & Buchheit — Science and Application of High-Intensity Interval Training (overreaching thresholds)
ACSM Guidelines for Exercise Testing and Prescription, 11th ed. (periodization)
rationale: Solves two compounding problems: (1) plans without scheduled deloads accumulate fatigue and either crush performance or cause injury; (2) zones drift over weeks of training and ad-hoc retesting is unreliable because users skip it. Bundling the test into a mandatory deload week guarantees both happen on the right cadence.
change_log: 2026-04-09: Initial threshold week protocol added. Goal-dependent cadence (4/6/8 weeks). Tests spread across the week for triathletes. Slide-on-skip rescheduling. Auto-recalculates zones via RUNNING_ZONE_CALCULATIONS.

title: Field tests for VDOT, FTP, and CSS — workout templates
principles: Centralized source of truth for the test workout templates so the planner, the workout viewer, and the result handler all reference the same definitions.
plan_rules: When the planner is generating a threshold week, it pulls the test workout structure from this module's tests array.
When a test workout is marked complete, the post-test modal pulls the user_inputs schema from this module to render the right entry fields.

tests.use_when: 5K race effort is contraindicated (injury return, masters, beginners)

evidence_sources: Coggan, A. & Allen, H. — Training and Racing with a Power Meter
Olbrecht, J. — The Science of Winning
rationale: Centralizes test workout templates so the planner, the workout viewer, and the result-handler all reference the same source of truth.

title: Run session type library — workout templates and generator rules
principles: 8 distinct run session types cover 95% of run intents without decision fatigue.
Long Run is its own category, distinct from Endurance.
Track Workouts (I-pace) and Speed Work (R-pace) are different stimuli — never merged.
Cruise intervals are the default Tempo prescription for amateurs.
plan_rules: When the user picks a session type, generate the workout via RUNNING_WORKOUT_GENERATOR using the matching template from session_types below.
Apply experience_scaling to rep counts, durations, and rest times.
Track Workout rotates weekly: rotation_index = (weeks_since_plan_start) mod 4.
Hills substitute for Track during a defined hill phase. Both in the same week is allowed only for advanced runners.

session_types.label: Easy / Recovery
session_types.why_text: Recovery and aerobic maintenance. Going harder today doesn't make you faster — it makes tomorrow worse.

session_types.label: Endurance Run
session_types.why_text: Building the aerobic engine that everything else runs on.

session_types.label: Long Run
session_types.why_text: Capillaries, fueling practice, mental durability. The single most important workout of the week.

session_types.label: Tempo / Threshold
session_types.why_text: Raising the pace you can sustain. The goal is comfortably hard, not race effort.

session_types.label: Track Workout
session_types.why_text: VO2max work. This is the session that makes race pace feel manageable.

session_types.label: Speed Work
session_types.why_text: Neuromuscular speed. Short, sharp, full recovery. Not aerobic — pure mechanics.

session_types.label: Hills
session_types.why_text: Power, strength, and injury resilience in one workout. Lower impact than flat track.

session_types.label: Fun / Social
session_types.purpose: Enjoyment, autonomy, mental break from structure
session_types.why_text: You earned this. No targets. The point is showing up because you want to.
session_types.instruction_text: Run by feel for {duration} minutes. No targets. Trail, treadmill, with a friend, with the dog. The point is showing up because you want to.

hard_constraints: Maximum 3 hard sessions per 7-day window. Hard = Tempo, Track, Speed Work, Hills, Long Run.
No back-to-back hard sessions. At least one Easy/Recovery or Endurance day must separate any two hard sessions.
No hard session within 24 hours of a Long Run.
Track Workout AND Speed Work in the same week is allowed only for advanced runners.
Hills substitute for Track during a hill phase. Both in the same week is allowed only for advanced runners.
Long Run frequency is capped at 1 per week, full stop.

conflict_resolution.trigger: user adds session on a day that already has a planned workout
conflict_resolution.on_replace: Remove planned workout. Save new workout. Trigger planner.rebalanceWeek(weekStartDate).
conflict_resolution.on_add: Save new workout as second session for that day. Run weekly stress check.

weekly_stress_check.trigger: before any save (replace or add path)
weekly_stress_check.rule: Count hard sessions in the resulting week. If > 3, surface secondary warning modal.
weekly_stress_check.modal_text: This would put {N} hard sessions in the week of {date}. The recommended max is 3. Hard sessions in this week so far: {list}. Are you sure?

evidence_sources: Daniels, J. — Daniels' Running Formula (5 intensities: E/M/T/I/R, cruise intervals)
Pfitzinger, P. & Douglas, S. — Advanced Marathoning (long run, hill phase, periodization)
Magness, S. — The Science of Running (track rotation, hill physiology)
Hudson, B. — Run Faster from the 5K to the Marathon (workout structure)
Hansons — Hansons Marathon Method (cumulative fatigue and long run capping)
rationale: Gives users a structured way to add custom sessions that respect the philosophy. The 8-option set covers 95% of run intents without decision fatigue. Track rotation prevents staleness. Conflict resolution and weekly stress check protect users from accidentally overtraining. Generator is fully deterministic — no API calls.
change_log: 2026-04-09: Initial 8-type session library and generator added. Track Workout rotates weekly across 800/1K/1200/ladder. Long Run kept separate from Endurance. Conflict resolution and weekly stress check defined.

title: Deterministic run workout generator — pure function from session type + zones
principles: Pure function. No API calls. No randomness except the rotation_index for track workouts (deterministic from weeks_since_plan_start).
plan_rules: Inputs: session_type_id, user_zones, experience_level, optional duration_override_min, weeks_since_plan_start.
Outputs: { workout: { title, type, is_hard, estimated_duration_min, phases, why_text, warnings } }.
If user_zones.vdot is missing, fall back to effort-based descriptions and surface a warning prompting the user to add a recent race result.

inputs.user_zones: from RUNNING_ZONE_CALCULATIONS — must include vdot, e_pace, m_pace, t_pace, i_pace, r_pace

workout.title: human-readable title
workout.warnings: array of any constraint warnings to surface to user

determinism: Pure function. No API calls. No randomness except the rotation_index for track workouts (which is deterministic from weeks_since_plan_start).

fallback_when_no_vdot.behavior: Generator falls back to effort-based descriptions instead of pace targets
fallback_when_no_vdot.prompt_user: For accurate pace targets, add a recent race result in your profile.

rationale: Centralizes the generation logic so the UI, the planner, and any future automation all call the same pure function.
